episodes.title: Introducing Yourself
episodes.description: Learn how to introduce yourself in English

transcript.text: Hello and welcome to our English learning podcast.
Today we're going to learn how to introduce yourself.
The most common way is to say: Hi, my name is...
You can also say: I'm... Nice to meet you.
When someone introduces themselves, you can respond with: Nice to meet you too.
Welcome back to our podcast. Today's topic is ordering food.
When you arrive at a restaurant, the waiter might ask: How many?
To order, you can say: I'd like... or Can I have...
At the end, ask for the bill: Could I have the check, please?

episodes.title: Asking for Directions
episodes.description: Learn how to ask for and give simple directions in English

transcript.text: Hello and welcome back! Today's lesson is about asking for directions.
If you're lost, you can say: Excuse me, can you help me? I'm looking for...
Common direction words: turn left, turn right, go straight, cross the road.
You can also ask: How far is it? Is it walking distance?
A helpful response might be: It's about 5 minutes on foot. You can't miss it.
Always thank the person: Thank you so much! That's very helpful.
Practice these phrases whenever you visit a new place!

episodes.title: Shopping for Clothes
episodes.description: Essential phrases for shopping and trying on clothes

transcript.text: Welcome! Today we're shopping for clothes in English.
When you enter a shop, staff may say: Can I help you? or Are you looking for anything in particular?
You can say: I'm just browsing, thanks. Or: Yes, I'm looking for a blue shirt.
To ask about sizes: Do you have this in a medium? or What sizes do you have?
To try clothes on: Can I try this on? The fitting rooms are usually at the back of the shop.
If you like it: I'll take it! or I'll buy this one, please.
If it doesn't fit: It's a bit too tight. Do you have a larger size?
Great job! Now you can shop confidently in English.

episodes.title: At the Doctor's
episodes.description: Learn how to describe symptoms and talk to a doctor

transcript.text: In today's episode, we'll practise visiting the doctor in English.
When you arrive, the receptionist will ask: Do you have an appointment?
You can say: Yes, I have an appointment at 3 PM. Or: I'd like to see a doctor today if possible.
The doctor will ask: What seems to be the problem? or How are you feeling?
Describe your symptoms: I have a headache. My throat is sore. I feel tired all the time.
If you need medicine: Can you prescribe something? The doctor will write a prescription.
Always say: Thank you, Doctor. See you at my follow-up appointment.

episodes.title: Talking About Your Family
episodes.description: Learn vocabulary and phrases for describing family members

transcript.text: Hello everyone! Today we're talking about family in English.
Core family members: mother, father, sister, brother, grandparents.
Extended family: aunt, uncle, cousin, niece, nephew.
To talk about your family: I have two brothers and one sister. My parents live in Hanoi.
You can describe personalities: My mum is very kind. My brother is funny.
Common questions: Are you close to your family? Do you have any siblings?
Practice talking about your own family in English every day!

episodes.title: Talking on the Phone
episodes.description: Key phrases for making and receiving calls in English

transcript.text: Today's topic is making phone calls in English — a skill many learners find challenging.
To answer the phone: Hello, this is... speaking. Or just: Hello?
To ask for someone: Could I speak to Sarah, please? Is Tom available?
If the person is unavailable: Could I leave a message? or Can you ask them to call me back?
To end the call politely: Thank you for calling. Goodbye! or I'll speak to you soon.
If you can't hear clearly: Sorry, could you speak up? I'm having trouble hearing you.
Practise these phrases with a friend or by calling English-speaking services.

episodes.title: Using Public Transport
episodes.description: Essential vocabulary for buses, trains and taxis

transcript.text: Welcome back! Today we'll learn how to use public transport in English.
At the bus stop: Which bus goes to the city centre? or Does this bus stop at the station?
Buying a train ticket: A single to Oxford, please. or Two return tickets to London.
Finding your platform: Which platform does the Manchester train leave from?
On the train: Is this seat taken? or Excuse me, I think this is my reservation.
Taking a taxi: Can you take me to the airport? How much will that be roughly?
Great work! You're now ready to travel confidently in any English-speaking city.
